speaker: patient
text: Good morning, Doctor. I’ve been feeling unusually tired and noticed some swelling in my ankles lately.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for coming in. Let’s start with your labs from yesterday—your blood glucose was elevated at 15.3 mmol/L.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin level was 113 g/L, which is on the lower end of normal but not critically low.

speaker: patient
text: I see. I’ve also been short of breath when walking up stairs, and my legs feel heavy.

speaker: patient
text: Yes—the lower limb edema has been getting worse over the past week, especially by evening.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s important. Given your history and current findings, your primary diagnosis remains Essential Hypertension.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re currently on Atorvastatin 20mg at bedtime for lipid management—still continuing that.

speaker: patient
text: Right, I’ve been taking it consistently. But my home BP readings have been high—I measured 168 over something yesterday.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic pressure today was 102 mmHg—definitely elevated and contributing to your symptoms.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, when I rechecked your hemoglobin just now, it came back at 99 g/L—lower than the 113 we saw earlier this week.

speaker: doctor
text: And your repeat glucose reading this morning was 11.8 mmol/L—still high, but down from 15.3.

speaker: doctor
text: Given the persistent hyperglycemia and your edema, I’m going to start you on Insulin glargine 10 units at bedtime to improve glycemic control.

speaker: patient
text: Okay—I’ll need some training on how to inject that safely.

speaker: doctor
text: Absolutely—we’ll schedule a diabetes nurse visit before you leave. Also, let’s monitor for signs of heart failure given the edema and hypertension.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been watching my salt intake, but the swelling hasn’t improved much even with elevation.

speaker: doctor
text: We may consider adding a low-dose diuretic after reviewing your renal function and electrolytes next week.

speaker: patient
text: Makes sense. By the way, my fatigue has been so bad I’ve had trouble concentrating at work.

speaker: doctor
text: That could be linked to both anemia and uncontrolled glucose—we’ll investigate iron studies and HbA1c next.

speaker: patient
text: One more thing—I brought today’s fingerstick result: hemoglobin came out as 113 again.

speaker: doctor
text: Ah—that matches the first reading we got, not the lower one from the lab draw. We’ll reconcile the discrepancy with a peripheral smear and reticulocyte count.
